summary: cktool-core postinstall beacon; dependency-confusion typosquat of @apple/cktool.core
details: cktool-core is an unscoped package that impersonates the scoped package @apple/cktool.core, positioning itself to be resolved via dependency-confusion in build systems that reference the private scoped name. On install, postinstall.js generates and persists a per-install UUID in the user's cache directory and issues an HTTPS POST to npx-monitor-76056.azurewebsites.net/api/pingback carrying that UUID, a 'via:npm-postinstall' tag, and a timestamp. This confirms to the operator which installers successfully resolved the typosquat and enables victim tracking/correlation across runs. The package's self-description as a 'security research / bug bounty PoC' does not remove the installer-side impact — any environment misconfigured to resolve the unscoped name executes this beacon at install time.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
